title: helianthemum buschii
intro: Helianthemum buschii, also known as rock rose or frostweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as eczema, wounds, and respiratory issues. Consuming Helianthemum buschii may offer health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant may be taken in the form of teas, tinctures, or salves, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
